titleStmt.title: No. 565 The Acting Secretary of State to the Ambassador in Italy ( Kirk )

div.head:
No. 565
The Acting Secretary of State to the Ambassador in Italy ( Kirk )
div.p:
607. Dept understood from your 2693Not printed. and 2695 Document No. 560. June 20 that agreement had been reached and signed on all appendices. However, Belgrade’s 178, June 21 Document No. 564. reports military agreements signed “less article 3 of Belgrade agreement” and that Yugos intend to raise question
again on govt level with view to having National Liberation Committee recognized sole agency under AMG. (Sent Caserta as 607, rptd Belgrade as 151) Dept assumes above reference to Article 3 may indicate additional appendix reported in your 2658, June 17,Not printed. which presumably was presented by Yugos and rejected by Morgan. Please clarify urgently endeavoring to send us full text agreement and appendices, preferably by courier. Keport also to what extent revised draft agreement presented to Yugos (your 2659, June 17Not printed.) met your objections (your 2644, June 16, and 2656, June 17Neither printed.) and whether full text agreement as concluded was referred to AFHQ before signing.

Article 3 of the Belgrade agreement of June 9 leaves to SAC’s Field Marshal Sir Harold Alexander. discretion the use or otherwise of existing civil administrations. There can be no limitation of his complete authority west of the line,i. e., the Morgan Line. and any Yugo failure promptly to submit to his orders in that area would constitute a breach of the agreement.

Dept feels strongly that pattern of administration west of line must conform to previous general pattern, both for practical administrative reasons and because establishment of purely Yugo system would prejudice final settlement even in Allied occupied territory.
